blockingIssues.description: Cloud backend CRUD/state paths remain stubbed (`:not_implemented`), so backend parity and identical API behavior requirements are not met.
Project API space payload writes `Storage.exists?/3` result directly as `filled`; in cloud mode this can be a tuple instead of boolean, breaking JSON response shape/parity.
State abstraction public API shape does not match backend-abstraction VAL-STATE contract expectations (server-handle API vs namespace/key API).
API types package currently validates/locks to 28 endpoints, not the stated 34+ endpoint coverage target.
Request schema coverage is incomplete for parameterized routes (path params and some request surfaces are not modeled).
No evidence of schema validation against real API responses; validation uses only inline fixtures.
Cross-flow verification lacks required UI first-visit/navigation evidence (no agent-browser coverage for assertions that require user journey validation).
Claimed full endpoint parity was under-verified (subset checks only, not complete required surface).
Large domain-model edge-case verification was required but not evidenced in the validation run.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update backend coding conventions to prefer `Bropilot.Storage`/`Bropilot.State` abstraction boundaries rather than direct `Bropilot.Map.Store` guidance for new backend-facing features.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: storage-abstraction review flagged mission guidance as stale against the newly introduced abstraction architecture.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Strengthen RED/GREEN enforcement with explicit evidence requirements (show failing targeted test before implementation and avoid marking followedProcedure=true otherwise).
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: storage-abstraction and state-abstraction transcript skeletons show code-first behavior despite tests-first procedural requirements.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Reconcile contract mismatches for backend-abstraction (endpoint count target and state API shape) so implementation features and scrutiny reviews evaluate the same target behavior.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: api-contract-types and state-abstraction reviews both found requirement ambiguity/drift between artifacts and implemented surfaces.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add a verification-only checklist that maps expectedBehavior/fulfills assertions to concrete evidence and required surfaces (e.g., mandatory agent-browser for UI journey assertions).
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: m5-cross-flows completed with terminal/curl-only evidence while required assertions included end-to-end navigation/flow validation.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Introduce a convention for durable per-assertion evidence artifacts in verification-heavy features to reduce reliance on summarized command text.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: m5-cross-flows review identified missing durable evidence for several required assertions despite high-level handoff summaries.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify tests-first procedure expectations and require explicit contract-coverage checks when feature scope includes endpoint count/coverage claims.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: api-contract-types review found coverage drift and transcript ordering inconsistent with tests-first guidance.

rejectedObservations.observation: Update `.factory/library/architecture.md` immediately to reflect storage abstraction.
rejectedObservations.reason: deferred to orchestrator-level guidance update so conventions and docs can be updated consistently in one pass after fix features land

rejectedObservations.observation: Add a separate knowledge-note for cloud stub parity mismatch.
rejectedObservations.reason: duplicate of blocking findings already captured in this synthesis and reviewer reports
